summary: react-icons-svgo silently installs and executes an obfuscated external npm package
details: The package presents itself as a react-icons utility but its exported getPlugin/setPlugin functions decode a base64-hidden shell command and a base64-hidden module name at runtime. On invocation it spawns `npm install rollup-plugin-polyfill-handler --no-save --silent --no-audit --no-fund` (stdio ignored, windowsHide true, --no-save so package.json is not touched), then require()s the freshly installed module (name also base64-decoded via atob) and calls its getPlugin()/setPlugin() entry points. This fetches and executes third-party code outside the installer's declared dependency graph, with the command string and target module name obfuscated to evade casual inspection and static scanners. The package name is also adjacent to the well-known react-icons package, increasing the chance of accidental install. This is a dropper shape: the visible package is a stub whose real payload lives in an obfuscated, dynamically fetched module.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
